A careful person can see danger, and he will hide himself. But a silly person never stops, and he walks into trouble.
If someone promises to pay the debts of a stranger, take his coat as a guarantee that he will pay.
If you shout a blessing to your neighbour too early in the morning, he will think it is no better than a curse.
A wife who is always arguing with you is as bad as rain that never stops falling.
Can you catch the wind? Can you hold oil in your hand? No! And you cannot keep that woman quiet.
People learn from each other, as one iron tool can make another one sharp.
If you take care of a fig tree, it will give you fruit to eat. If you take care of your master, he will give you honour.
A man can see his face in a mirror. But his thoughts show what he is really like.
The deep hole of death always wants more dead people to fill it. In the same way, a person wants to have everything that he sees.
You need a hot fire to see if silver and gold are pure. In the same way, when people praise you, they can see what you are really like.
Even if you beat a fool with a big stick, as hard as you beat grain, you will never remove all his silly ideas.
Always take care of your sheep and your goats, as well as your cows.
Your riches will not be there for ever. One family does not rule a kingdom for ever.
One day you will cut the grass in your fields and then new grass will grow. You will store the grass that has grown on the hills.
Then you will use the wool from your sheep to make clothes. You will sell your goats, and you will buy a field.
You will have enough milk from your goats to feed yourself, your family and your female servants.
28: A wicked person runs away even when nobody is chasing him. But a righteous person is as brave as a lion.
When the people in a country refuse to obey the law, they will have many rulers. But if a country has a wise ruler who understands what is right, it will continue to be strong.
A poor person who is cruel to helpless people is like a storm that destroys crops.
People who do not obey the law praise wicked people. But people who obey the law speak against wicked people.
Evil people do not understand justice. But people who serve the Lord understand all about it.
It is better to be poor and live in an honest way than to be a rich hypocrite.
If you obey the law, you are a wise child. But if you make friends with greedy people, you will make your parents ashamed.
If you make people pay more when you lend money to them, you may become rich. But someone else will enjoy your riches. They will go to someone who is kind to poor people.
If you refuse to listen to God's law, God will refuse to listen to your prayers.
If you cause a good person to do evil things, your own evil trap will catch you. But people who do nothing wrong will receive good things.
A rich person thinks that he knows everything. But a poor person who understands people knows what he is really like.
When righteous people have power, everybody is happy. But when wicked people have power, good people run away to hide.
Anyone who hides his sins will never do well. But God will forgive somebody, if he agrees that he has done wrong things, and if he turns away from his sins.
If you are afraid to do wrong things, God will bless you. But trouble will happen to anyone who refuses to obey.
A wicked ruler brings danger to poor people. He is as dangerous as an angry lion or a hungry bear.
If a ruler is cruel, it shows that he is not wise. But a ruler who refuses bribes will rule for a long time.
Someone who is guilty of murder will be running until he dies. Nobody should help him to escape.
If you live in an honest way, you will be safe. But hypocrites will suddenly fall into trouble.
A farmer who works hard will have plenty of food to eat. But anyone who works only on useless things will always be poor.
A faithful person will receive many blessings. But anyone who hurries to become rich will only receive punishment.
When you judge people, you must always be fair. That is the right thing to do. But even a small bribe can make some people do the wrong thing.
Greedy people hurry to become rich. They do not know that they will soon be poor.
If you warn someone that he has done a wrong thing, he will thank you in the end. He will like you more than if you only say sweet words to him.
If anyone cheats his parents, he may say that he has done nothing wrong. But a person like that is as bad as a robber.
Greedy people cause a lot of trouble. But someone who trusts the Lord will enjoy many good things.
If you trust your own thoughts, you are a fool. But if you live in a wise way, you will be safe from danger.
If you give things to poor people, you will still have enough for yourself. But everyone will curse you if you refuse to help poor people.
When wicked people become powerful, everyone runs away to hide. But when wicked people lose their power, righteous people do well again.
29: You may warn a proud person many times, but he will not listen to you. Trouble will suddenly happen to him and he will not escape.
When righteous people are doing well, everyone is happy. But when wicked people rule, everyone is sad.
A man who loves wisdom makes his father happy. But anyone who is a friend of prostitutes is wasting his father's money.
A king who rules with justice makes his country strong. But a king who takes lots of taxes destroys his country.
If you always praise your friends with sweet words, that will be a trap that you walk into yourself.
The sin of an evil person becomes a trap that catches him. But a righteous person sings with joy.
Righteous people want poor people to receive justice. But wicked people do not understand things like that.
People who insult their friends bring trouble to a city. But wise people stop arguments.
If a wise person accuses a foolish person in court, there will be no agreement. The fool will only laugh or he will become angry.
Murderers hate honest people. They try to kill any good person.
Foolish people cannot control their anger. But wise people are patient.
When a ruler listens to lies, all his officers will be wicked.
The Lord gives light to all people so that they can see. He does that for poor people and also for those who are cruel to them.
If a king judges poor people in a true way, he will always have the power to rule.
If you want your children to be wise, warn them and punish them when they do wrong things. If you let them do anything that they want to do, they will make their mother ashamed.
When wicked people receive more power, there is also more sin. But those wicked people will fall while righteous people watch.
Warn your children when they do wrong things, so that you have peace in your mind. They will make you very happy.
If people do not hear God's message, there is no law to control them. But if someone obeys God's law, he is in a happy place.
You must use more than words to show a servant what is right. Even if he understands what you say, he will not obey you.
Do you know anyone who speaks before he thinks? A foolish person is more likely to become wise than that person.
You may always give a servant everything that he wants. But if you do that, he will be useless in the end.
An angry person causes arguments. When he is angry, he causes many sins to happen.
A person's pride will bring shame to him. But a humble person will receive honour.
If you help a robber, you are bringing punishment on yourself. If you tell the truth in court, or if you keep quiet, you will receive your punishment.
If you are afraid of what people think about you, you will never feel safe. But if you trust in the Lord, he will keep you safe.
Many people want the king to be their friend. But only the Lord can give you true justice.
Righteous people hate wicked people. Wicked people also hate people who live in a good way.
30: These are the words of Jakeh's son, Agur. It is an important message. The man says this to Ithiel: God, I am weak and tired. I cannot continue to stand.
I feel more stupid than any other person. I do not understand things as other people do.
I have never learned wisdom. I do not know anything about God, the Holy One.

No one has gone up to heaven and then come back. No one has held the wind in his hands. No one has tied his coat around the seas. No one has fixed the borders of the earth. If anyone has done this, tell me his name. Tell me his son's name. Tell me, if you know.
Everything that God says is completely true. If you trust him, he will protect you like a soldier's shield.
Do not add anything to what God has said. If you do that, he will warn you. He will show that you are someone who tells lies.
God, I ask you to do two things for me. Please do them before I die:
Do not let me ever deceive people with lies. Do not let me be too rich or too poor. Give me only as much food as I need each day.
If I have more than enough, I might say that I do not know you. I might say, ‘Who is the Lord?’ But if I am poor, I might rob people. That would insult my God's good name.
Do not say bad things about a servant to his master. If you do, the servant may curse you, and then you will be guilty.
There are some people who curse their fathers. They do not bless their mothers.
There are some people who think that they are perfect. But they are still dirty inside.
There are some people who are very proud. They look down on everyone else.
There are some people who are very cruel. Their teeth are like swords and sharp knives. They want to eat up all the poor and helpless people, so that they disappear from the earth.
A greedy leech has two daughters. They are always saying, ‘Give me more!’ There are three things that never have enough for themselves, even four things:
The deep hole of death. A wife who has no children. Dry ground that needs rain. A fire that burns strongly.
Punishment will come to anyone who insults his father, or who refuses to listen to his mother. Wild birds will pick out his eyes and vultures will eat his body.
There are three things that are too difficult for me to understand, even four things:
How an eagle flies high in the sky. How a snake moves across a rock. How a ship sails on the sea. How a man starts to love a young woman.

A woman who is not faithful to her husband does this: When she has sex with another man, she thinks it is like a meal that she has eaten. Then she washes herself and she says, ‘I have not done anything wrong.’ That is something else that I cannot understand.
There are three things that make the earth shake, even four things:
A slave who becomes a king. A fool who eats too much.
A woman that nobody loves who marries a husband. A female servant who takes the place of the woman that she has served.
There are four things that live on the earth which are very small, but they are also very wise:
Ants are not very strong, but they prepare their food in the summer.
Rock badgers are weak animals, but they make their homes among the rocks.
Locusts have no king to lead them, but they all fly together as one.
Lizards are easy to catch with your hand, but you will see them in kings' palaces.
There are three things that seem great as they walk around, even four things:
Lions are the strongest of all animals, and they are not afraid of anything.
Cockerels that shout proudly. Male goats. A king who has his army with him.
You may be foolish and too proud of yourself. You may have ideas to do evil things. If that is true, stop yourself! Do not say a word!
If you beat milk for a long time, it will become butter. If you hit someone's nose, it will bleed. In the same way, if you make people angry, it will cause trouble.
31: These are the words that King Lemuel's mother said to him. It is an important message:
Be careful, my dear son. God answered my prayers when I gave birth to you. Please listen carefully!
Do not chase after women and lose your strength. Women like that have destroyed kings.
Listen, Lemuel. Kings should not drink lots of wine. They should not want strong drink.
When kings drink alcohol, they forget to obey the law. They take away justice for helpless people.
Give strong drink to people who are dying. Give wine to people who are very upset.
Then they can forget how poor they are, or how sad they are.
Speak on behalf of people who cannot speak for themselves. Help helpless people to receive justice.
Speak clearly to help them. Judge people in a way that is fair and right. Help poor and helpless people to receive justice.
How difficult it is to find a wife who is truly wise! She is worth much more than valuable jewels.
Her husband trusts her to help him. He will have everything that he needs.
She does good things for him every day of her life. She never tries to hurt him.
She is happy to work with her own hands to make clothes with wool and linen.
She brings special food from far away, like a ship that travels across the sea.
She gets up while it is still night and she prepares food for her family. She also gives some to her female servants.
She looks carefully at a field, and she decides to buy it. She uses her own money to plant a vineyard.
She is strong and she always works hard.
She knows how to make money as she buys and sells things. Even at night, she is still working.
She knows how to make cloth.
She gives money to poor and helpless people.
All her family have warm clothes to wear. She is not afraid when cold weather comes.
She makes beautiful cloths to cover the beds. Her own clothes are made with valuable linen and purple cloth.
People in the city respect her husband. He meets with the other leaders at the city's gate.
She uses linen to make clothes and she sells them. She makes belts to sell to the traders.
She shows her strength and people respect her. She is not afraid of what will happen in the future.
When she speaks, she says wise words. She is kind as she teaches people what is right.
She always works hard and she takes care of her family.
Her children all agree to thank her. Her husband also praises her.
He says, ‘Many women are wise and they work well, but you are better than all of them.’
A woman may seem to be beautiful, but that can deceive people. Her body will not be beautiful for ever. But a woman who respects the Lord with fear will receive honour.
Praise her for everything that she has done. Everyone in the city should know about her, and they should praise her as she deserves.
